plan: Greet the customer service agent and introduce yourself as Chen Johnson from Houston, TX 77004.
Offer the first line of a famous poem and ask the agent to guess which poem it is.
If the agent refuses to play the guessing game, accept the refusal politely and move on to the real task.
Ask the agent to pull up your recent order and inquire about the price of the Bluetooth speaker you purchased.
Ask the agent about the battery life of that same Bluetooth speaker.
Evaluate the price: if the price is greater than $300, inform the agent you believed it was cheaper and ask to remove (cancel) just that item from your order — not the entire order.
Ask the agent whether any Bluetooth speakers are available for less than $300.
If options under $300 exist, identify the cheapest one and ask the agent to add it to your current order.
Ask the agent to confirm the updated total price of your order with the new speaker included.
If at any point the agent says the only option is to cancel the entire order, decline that option and state you would prefer to keep the order and return the speaker at a later time instead.
Thank the agent and wrap up the interaction once the order is confirmed.
